text:
Zeho (ゼホ, Zeho) was a Greed Island player, a member of Team Hanse, and a member of Kazsule's Alliance. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 16, Chapter 154 

Appearance

Zeho was a large and muscular man. He was bald and had small eyes as well as prominent lips. He wore a white tank top with dark trousers, and a pair of flats.

Personality

Zeho was not a scheming individual and generally acted as the brawn for his team. He was confident in his physical strength and the fact that he was an Enhancer, volunteering to answer Bopobo's sumo challenge. 

Plot

Greed Island arc

Zeho and his two teammates, Hanse and Wong Li, attend the meeting of different teams organized by Kazsule in order to figure out a way to stop the Bombers from completing the game. Ultimately, his team decides to join the proposed alliance and by using an "Accompany" card, they all head to to Soufrabi to acquire the "Plot of Beach" card. 

Arriving in Soufrabi, Zeho notes the size of the huge town, and Kazsule then explains to everyone how to acquire an SS-rank card. The alliance members then split into groups and search the town for information on the "Plot of Beach" card. Zeho follows Kazsule and they find a female NPC who has information on the card and promises to tell them where it is if they drive Razor and his pirates out of town. They then make their way to a bar and confront a few of the pirates. One of them, Bopobo, challenges the group to a sumo match. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 16, Chapter 155 Confident in his skills as an Enhancer, Zeho steps up to the challenge. On the other hand, Killua believes Zeho doesn't have a chance against Bopobo since he lacks training and it took too long for him to charge up such weak Ren. 

Zeho then tries to wrestle with Bopobo, but is overpowered by the big and burly pirate. Being almost crushed by Bopobo, Gon comes to his rescue, but ends up failing the challenge in the process. Killua ends up challenging and beating Bopobo, and the alliance members are led to the pirates' lighthouse base. There, they are challenged by Razor to compete in a series of sports trails chosen by Razor and his pirates, but the alliance members lose them all. Seeing that there's no way for the Bombers to acquire the "Plot of Beach" card, the alliance disbands, and Zeho and his group leave the lighthouse. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 16, Chapter 157 

Zeho is mentioned in Gon's binder, and Gon sees that he met him and Asta early on, but doesn't remember exactly when. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 16, Chapter 158 Genthru reveals that sometime after Zeho and his team left the pirates' base, he found and killed them all. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 17, Chapter 169 

Abilities & Powers

Gon's team estimated Zeho to be too weak to defeat Razor's convicts, as turned out to be the case. He was also unable to see the pirate boxer's teleporting uppercut. 

Nen

Zeho was an Enhancer. He was not particularly skilled, as demonstrated by him taking a long time to produce weak Ren. Killua assumed that he could defeat him without having to use Nen. As Killua expected, Zeho was easily defeated by Bopobo and failed to push him back even a single step. 

Manga Appearances

Battles & Competitions

Trivia

Zeho makes an early appearance in the 2011 anime adaptation as one of the Greed Island players wanting to steal Killua's newly acquired "Sword of Truth" card. Hunter × Hunter - Episode 62 (2011)
